parameters.name: Saturation
parameters.description: Amount of saturation applied to the signal where 0 is none and 1 is maximum

parameterGroups.label: Gain Staging
parameterGroups.rationale: PreGain drives the signal into the saturator, PostGain compensates for the added level

parameters.name: Pre Gain
parameters.description: Input gain in decibels applied before saturation

parameters.name: Post Gain
parameters.description: Output gain in decibels applied after saturation

parameters.name: Wet Amount
parameters.description: Dry/wet mix where 0 is fully dry and 1 is fully wet

standaloneModChains.name: Saturation Modulation
standaloneModChains.note: Modulates the saturation amount. No parameterIndex - standalone chain.

gaps.question: What is the exact processing order? Is it: input -> preGain -> saturation -> postGain -> wet/dry mix -> output?
gaps.context: Need to confirm the gain staging order for the pseudo-code.

gaps.question: What waveshaping function is used? tanh, soft clip, polynomial? How does the Saturation parameter control the curve shape?
gaps.context: The waveshaping algorithm determines the harmonic character.

gaps.question: How is the Saturation Modulation chain applied? Does it multiply the Saturation parameter value, or replace it? Per-sample or per-block?
gaps.context: Users need to understand modulation resolution and behaviour.

gaps.question: PreGain range is 0-24 dB (boost only). Is 0 dB truly unity, or is there an offset? PostGain range is -24 to 0 dB (cut only). Verify these ranges match actual behaviour.
gaps.context: Unusual that PreGain cannot cut and PostGain cannot boost.

gaps.question: How is WetAmount applied? Is it a linear crossfade between dry and saturated signal?
gaps.context: Need to document the mixing behaviour accurately.

gaps.question: Is the saturation per-sample? Any oversampling? Does the waveshaping function vary in cost with the Saturation parameter?

notes: Saturator has a standalone modulation chain (Saturation Modulation) with no parameterIndex. PreGain is boost-only (0-24 dB), PostGain is cut-only (-24 to 0 dB), suggesting a gain-staging workflow where you drive the signal in and compensate on the way out.
